old_claim.note: Crop Rotation (Three-Field System) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained between Crop Landrace Selection and Crop Rotation (Three-Field System); Crop landrace selection can occur independently of the European three-field rotation system.

source_shape.source_locator: Article defines the three-field system as a Middle Ages European agricultural organization that rotated winter crops, spring crops, and fallow land.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Crop landrace selection can occur independently of the European three-field rotation system.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports the scoped Crop Landrace Selection chronology or adjacent technology without requiring Crop Rotation (Three-Field System) as a prerequisite.

ontology_before: Crop Landrace Selection treated Crop Rotation (Three-Field System) as a direct prerequisite.
ontology_after: Crop Landrace Selection no longer directly depends on Crop Rotation (Three-Field System); the dependency boundary is narrowed to operationally necessary predecessors.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: crop_landrace_selection must not directly depend on crop_rotation_three_field unless a narrower source-backed node scope is introduced.
would_reject_if: A source shows crop_landrace_selection is intentionally scoped only to cases that require crop_rotation_three_field.
The graph splits crop_landrace_selection and reserves this id for a narrower technology directly dependent on crop_rotation_three_field.
A stronger source demonstrates that crop_rotation_three_field is chronologically and causally necessary for crop_landrace_selection.
short_reason: Crop landrace selection can occur independently of the European three-field rotation system.
